error_signature: n8n started as a new instance, ignoring existing data
title: n8n resets to fresh install after setting N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY
symptom: After setting the N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY in Docker Compose, n8n behaves as a fresh install, losing all workflows and credentials.
applies_when: Using Docker Compose to run n8n
not_this_if: Using the default configuration without environment variables
No changes made to the encryption key
root_cause: The issue arises when the encryption key contains special characters that are misinterpreted by Docker Compose during variable substitution. This can lead to n8n not recognizing the key correctly, causing it to initialize as a new instance.
fix_steps: Edit the .env file to wrap the encryption key in single quotes
Escape any dollar signs in the key by replacing them with double dollar signs ($$)
Alternatively, set the key directly in the docker-compose.yml file instead of using the .env file
Restart the n8n instance with 'docker compose down' and 'docker compose up -d'
agent_instruction: Wrap the encryption key in single quotes and escape any dollar signs in the .env file, or set it directly in the compose file.
